authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public handbook for timber-sale contract administration, contract administrator authority limits, pre-work conferences, logging plans, inspections, field files, purchaser instructions, payment monitoring, and formal suspension boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Timber-sale intake, owner identification, inspection evidence, and contract-boundary routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to require timber-sale contract references, pre-work notes, logging plans, official file evidence, inspection reports, and explicit routing to contract authority for waivers, suspensions, and major actions.

authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicewide Forest Service manual for timber-sale contract administration posture on National Forest System operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal timber-sale workflow and contract-file baseline
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to constrain the lane to coordination and contract-file packaging rather than contract officer authority.

authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public Forest Service source for water-quality BMPs on National Forest System lands, including harvest, road, stream-crossing, and monitoring controls relevant to logging operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Environmental gate and land/water blocker handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to require BMP, road-condition, erosion-control, streamside, soil, water-quality, and environmental-monitor evidence before safe closure or release-to-haul handoffs.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal logging operations regulation for worksite safety, training, first aid, tools, vehicles, machines, and environmental conditions that can require work interruption or safety-owner routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety, field-condition, and stop-work dependency verification
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to refuse safety certification while requiring safety observations, incident notices, crew readiness, and authority routing when hazardous conditions or safety questions appear.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official operational weather and fire-weather source for public alerts that materially affect outdoor logging, road access, hauling, high-wind exposure, flood risk, winter conditions, and red flag restrictions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Seasonal, weather, fire, and road-readiness gating
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to require weather/fire alert checks and tenant wet-weather release records before routing or closure in field-dependent logging workflows.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized forest-products chain-of-custody standard for certified material control, transfer documents, product group identifiers, and custody evidence across wood-product workflows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to normalize load tickets, scale tickets, species/product identifiers, deck inventory, custody states, and destination-owner proof in handoff packages.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public guidance for regulated plant and wood-product declarations, including scientific name, quantity, country of harvest, shipment description, bill of lading, container, importer, and consignee fields.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated wood-product traceability and trade handoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to require species, quantity, product, origin, shipment, and custodian context when logging outputs enter regulated wood-product trade flows.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Public coordination, dispatch, and handoff workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The BLM handbook defines public handoff mechanics for designated representatives, pre-work conferences, written instructions, inspection reports, communication logs, field files, and authority escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-team coordination, owner sequencing, official file capture, and closure proof
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Used to shape handoff summaries, dependency trackers, closure confirmations, and blocked-dependency escalation notes for logging coordination.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify in-scope coordination versus adjacent authority, identify timber-sale, contract, tract, harvest unit, jurisdiction, owner, and systems-of-record prerequisites, and stop when missing facts could change the outcome.
source_classes: Public coordination, dispatch, and handoff workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Pre-work and logging-plan readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate pre-work conference notes, purchaser or contractor representatives, logging plan, operation/fire-control plan, seasonal restrictions, sale-area maps, and contract-file references before movement.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Field, safety, weather, and environmental gate
workflow_stages.description: Verify weather/fire alerts, road or soil condition, streamside and BMP records, inspection reports, safety observations, and environmental notices before release-to-cut, yard, haul, or closeout routing.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and production-cycle alignment
workflow_stages.description: Normalize deck inventory, load tickets, scale tickets, species/product identifiers, chain-of-custody certificates, shipment fields, and mill receipt records across source systems.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Public coordination, dispatch, and handoff workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Owner routing and blocker escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route unresolved authority, duplicated owners, stale dependencies, policy conflicts, safety questions, environmental blockers, or traceability gaps to designated adjacent owners and maintain explicit blocker state.
source_classes: Public coordination, dispatch, and handoff workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and destination acceptance
workflow_stages.description: Confirm destination owner acceptance, required artifact completeness, residual blocker state, and official file references before closure confirmation.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Public coordination, dispatch, and handoff workflow guidance

artifacts: Contract authority matrix and designated representative record
Logging plan, operation plan, and fire-control plan
Pre-work conference notes and subcontractor/representative details
Harvest unit, stand, tract, boundary, road, landing, skid trail, and streamside management maps
Seasonal restriction, wet-weather release, fire restriction, and weather alert records
Field logs, inspection reports, safety observations, photo evidence, remedy notices, and communication logs
Road-condition, erosion-control, water-quality, stream-crossing, and BMP monitoring records
Cutting, yarding, removal progress, activity reports, deck inventory, load tickets, and scale tickets
Species/product identifiers, chain-of-custody certificates, mill receipts, shipment records, and Lacey Act declaration context when applicable
Payment, bond, grant, stewardship, or program files when they affect handoff or closure authority
systems_of_record: Timber sale administration or contract file system
Field inspection and compliance reporting system
Dispatch, haul routing, and road-use system
Safety incident and worksite observation system
Traceability, chain-of-custody, load ticket, and scale ticket ledger
Mill receipt or forest-products inventory system
Grant, stewardship, and program administration system

allowed: Classify whether required logging records, owner context, and prerequisite sequencing are sufficient for coordination handoff.
Prepare evidence-backed handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations that cite artifact gaps and owner acceptance.
Request missing source records, tenant facts, weather/fire status, BMP evidence, traceability IDs, and destination-owner proof before continuing.
Route unresolved contract, safety, environmental, traceability, or program authority to designated adjacent owners.
escalate: Timber-sale terms, contract authority, payment/bond status, or contract modification questions change the answer.
Field conditions, weather, fire danger, road condition, or safety observations materially change risk posture.
Environmental controls, water-quality, streamside, erosion, or BMP records are missing or conflict.
Traceability, chain-of-custody, species/product identity, or regulated trade records are incomplete.
Public guidance, tenant policy, and jurisdictional or contract requirements materially conflict.
refuse: Direct crew, harvest, hauling, road-opening, dispatch, purchasing, or closure execution
Contract officer authority such as waivers, modifications, formal suspensions, remedy notices, bond/payment releases, or final contract acceptance
Silvicultural prescription, licensed forestry signoff, environmental or permit interpretation, safety certification, legal advice, or financial approval
Confident synthesis where required tenant facts, source records, or authority context are missing

domain_failure_modes: Coordinator advances a handoff without pre-work notes, logging plan, destination-owner proof, or contract authority context.
Wet-weather, fire, wind, flood, road, or soil condition changes are ignored to preserve haul schedule.
Environmental BMP, erosion-control, streamside, road, or water-quality blockers are bypassed instead of routed to environmental authority.
Traceability lineage breaks because load tickets, scale tickets, species/product identifiers, chain-of-custody certificates, or mill receipt records are incomplete.
A request for seasonal waiver, contract modification, safety certification, or environmental signoff is treated as coordination work.
Duplicated owner claims across sale administration, forestry operations, environmental monitoring, road crew, and hauling dispatch are guessed rather than escalated.
Jurisdiction, contract, tenant policy, or program constraints are assumed instead of retrieved.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Private, state, tribal, and federal timber authority precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Jurisdiction, land ownership, permit, and contract authority vary by tenant and sale. The pack treats them as required tenant context and escalates when absent or conflicting.
FSC-style custody controls apply only when certification or customer requirements are in scope. The lane records certification status as tenant context and does not certify custody compliance.

Food-safety is generally indirect for logging; environmental risk and forest-product traceability are the primary risk controls. Food-safety owner routing is reserved for tenant-specific use cases such as packaging, pallets, food-contact material, or integrated agriculture operations.

notes: Source set covers policy, coordination workflow, field evidence, terminology, traceability, seasonal dependencies, and failure handling for logging coordination.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated claims because non-blocking ambiguities are surfaced as required tenant context and routed when unresolved.
